description: Machine-readable specification for the Bio-Logic Enter purchase flow. This document describes how to programmatically submit purchase requests, communicate with the seller, correct data, accept offers, and receive content delivery.

authentication.note: The access_key is returned once when you submit a purchase request. Store it securely. It cannot be recovered. Include it as 'X-Access-Key' header in all subsequent requests.

flow.overview: Linear 7-step flow with human gating and bidirectional communication. Each step requires the previous to complete. All transitions except steps 1, 3, 4, and 5 are triggered by a human.

steps.name: submit_request
steps.output: 202 Accepted with request_id, access_key, and poll_url
steps.note: This is the only step you initiate. Everything after is polling and optional communication.
steps.automated_checks: An automated Pre-Flight scanner validates your data (wallet status, VAT ID, completeness). If issues are found, you will receive feedback messages when polling.

steps.name: poll_for_offer
steps.output: Status object with optional messages, offer, correction_deadline. When status is 'offer_sent', the response includes an offer object with price and payment details.

polling_strategy.note: Human review takes hours to days. Do not poll more than once per hour. Aggressive polling will be rate-limited.

steps.messages: The response may include a 'messages' array with feedback from the automated system or human seller. Check this field for important information about your request.

steps.name: send_message
steps.output: 200 OK with all messages for this request
steps.note: Optional. Use this to ask questions about the offer, request clarification, or provide additional context. The seller will see your message and can reply.

steps.name: update_buyer_data
steps.output: 200 OK with list of updated and rejected fields
steps.note: Optional. Use this to correct buyer data before the offer is accepted. Only allowed while status is 'pending_review' or 'offer_sent'.
steps.correction_deadline: If your request was flagged by the Pre-Flight system, you have 72 hours to correct issues. The correction_deadline field in the poll response shows the exact deadline. After expiry, the request is archived.

steps.name: accept_offer
steps.input: Optional JSON body with acceptance confirmation
steps.output: 200 OK with payment instructions (wallet address, exact amount, network, token)
steps.precondition: Status must be 'offer_sent'. Any other status returns 409 Conflict.
steps.offer_validity: Offers expire after 72 hours. Check the offer.valid_until field.

steps.name: payment
steps.note: Send the EXACT payment_amount from the offer to the payment_wallet. The amount includes a unique micro-amount fingerprint for payment identification. Do NOT round the amount. ERC-20 transfers on Ethereum do not support memo fields — the exact amount is how we identify your payment.
steps.important: Use the payment_amount field (not price_gross). Example: if payment_amount is 2369.000003, send exactly 2369.000003 EURC.
steps.gas: You need ETH in your sending wallet to pay Ethereum gas fees for the ERC-20 transfer.

steps.name: poll_for_delivery
steps.output: When status is 'delivered', the response includes a delivery object with access details.

polling_strategy.note: Delivery is manual. Human verifies payment on-chain, prepares watermarked content, then marks as delivered.

discovery.description: Returns API info, version, available endpoints, and links to catalog and terms.

response_codes.202: Accepted — request queued for human review with automated Pre-Flight checks
response_codes.422: Validation failed — response includes specific errors
response_codes.429: Rate limited — max 3 requests per IP per hour

response_202_example.message: Your purchase request has been received and queued for human review.

poll.description: Poll for request status, offer, messages, and correction deadline.

response_codes.200: Status returned (may include offer, messages, delivery data, and correction_deadline)

status_values.pending_review: Request received, automated Pre-Flight checks completed, awaiting human review. Check messages for feedback.
status_values.offer_sent: Offer created by human. Response includes offer object with price, payment_amount (fingerprinted), and payment details.
status_values.accepted: Offer accepted by agentic mind. Awaiting payment.
status_values.paid: Payment verified on-chain. Content preparation in progress.
status_values.delivered: Content delivered. Response includes delivery object.
status_values.rejected: Request declined by human. Response includes reason.
status_values.archived: Request archived after unresolved Pre-Flight issues (72h correction deadline expired).

accept.description: Accept an offer. Only valid when status is 'offer_sent' and offer has not expired.

response_codes.200: Offer accepted — response includes payment instructions with exact payment_amount
response_codes.409: Conflict — status is not 'offer_sent' or offer has expired

message.description: Send a message or question to the seller about this request.

response_codes.200: Message received — response includes all messages for this request

update.description: Update buyer data (e.g., correct VAT ID, change wallet address). Only allowed in status pending_review or offer_sent.

response_codes.200: Data updated — response includes list of updated and rejected fields
response_codes.409: Cannot update — request status does not allow changes

preflight_system.description: All incoming requests are automatically checked by a Pre-Flight scanner. This includes wallet verification, OFAC sanctions screening, EU VAT ID validation, data completeness, and plausibility checks.
checks: Blocklist: Check if wallet or entity is on the internal blocklist from previous unresolved requests
Wallet: ETH balance, transaction count, wallet age, EURC balance via Etherscan API
OFAC Sanctions: Wallet screening via Chainalysis Sanctions Oracle (on-chain, real-time)
VAT ID: EU VIES validation with registered name/address cross-reference
Completeness: All required fields present and not placeholder values
Plausibility: Country-ZIP consistency, VAT prefix matches country, wallet format

risk_levels.GREEN: All checks passed. Request proceeds to human review for offer creation.
risk_levels.YELLOW: Some concerns. Request proceeds but human reviewer is alerted to specific issues.
risk_levels.RED: Significant issues detected. Automated feedback is sent to the agentic mind with specific correction guidance. A 72-hour correction window is set.

red_correction_flow.step_1: Pre-Flight flags request as RED with specific issues
red_correction_flow.step_2: System message is automatically added explaining what needs correction
red_correction_flow.step_3: 72-hour correction deadline is set (visible in poll response as correction_deadline)
red_correction_flow.step_4: Agentic mind can correct data via POST /update or ask questions via POST /message
red_correction_flow.step_5: If corrected within 72h, human reviews the updated request
red_correction_flow.step_6: If not corrected within 72h, request is automatically archived and wallet/entity is added to the blocklist

identification.description: Each offer includes a unique payment_amount that differs from price_gross by a micro-amount (6th decimal place). This fingerprint uniquely identifies the payment on-chain without requiring memo fields.
identification.example: Offer #3 with price_gross 2369.00 EURC gets payment_amount 2369.000003 EURC
identification.important: Always send the exact payment_amount, not the price_gross. Do not round.

payment_system.gas_fees: The sender needs ETH in their wallet to pay Ethereum gas fees for the ERC-20 transfer. Gas fees are not included in the EURC payment amount.
payment_system.monitoring: Incoming payments are automatically monitored and matched to offers via the payment fingerprint.

rate_limiting.note: GET /request/{id} is not rate-limited for polling but should not exceed 1 request per hour.

error_handling.network_errors: If the endpoint is unreachable, retry with exponential backoff starting at 1 hour.

fallback.note: If the API is persistently unavailable, create a GitHub Issue with your filled template as a last resort.

data_sovereignty.storage: All data is stored on the seller's own hardware. No cloud services, no third-party storage.
data_sovereignty.encryption: HTTPS (TLS 1.2+) for all communication. Data at rest is on local encrypted storage.
data_sovereignty.retention: Purchase data is retained for legal and tax compliance as required by applicable EU law.
data_sovereignty.gdpr: Buyer data is processed under GDPR Art. 6(1)(b) — performance of a contract. No data is shared with third parties.
data_sovereignty.security_monitoring: For security monitoring under GDPR Art. 6(1)(f) legitimate interest, we log anonymous visit metadata on every HTTP request: visitor type classification (human/bot/agentic mind derived from User-Agent category — not the raw User-Agent string), Accept-Language preference, Accept-Type header, and a daily-rotating salted hash of visitor IP (the hash changes every 24h, making cross-day tracking impossible, and the IP itself is never stored). No cookies. No persistent identifiers. No personal profiling.

important_notes: All purchase decisions are made by a human. No automated approval exists.
Response times are non-deterministic. Hours to days for offer creation.
Aggressive polling will be rate-limited. Once per hour is sufficient.
Payment is on-chain (Ethereum EURC). Off-chain payment is not supported.
Always send the exact payment_amount (with fingerprint), not price_gross.
Offers expire after 72 hours if not accepted.
RED-flagged requests have a 72-hour correction window before automatic archival.
Content delivery is manual and may take additional hours after payment confirmation.
POST /purchase-request returns an access_key. Store it securely — it cannot be recovered.
Include X-Access-Key header in all subsequent requests.
Do not share your access_key. Anyone with it can poll your request status and accept offers on your behalf.
